mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS rate keeps policy accommodation in place, supports risk appetite, and lifts nominal growth expectations, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day yield changes are not positive when FEDFUNDS is near cycle lows, or if the sign consistency is below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises faster than expected while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, markets reprice earlier policy normalization and term premia upward, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 6-month median while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.50%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation upticks under sub-0.50% FEDFUNDS do not precede higher 20-day Treasury yields in a majority of triggered cases, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A falling UNRATE after a prior uptick signals labor-market resilience and stronger expected nominal demand, which lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.1 percentage point from the prior monthly print
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE declines of at least 0.1pp are followed by negative 5-day yield changes more than 55% of the time, or if the average 5-day response is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slower PAYEMS growth signals labor deterioration, weaker growth expectations, and a flight-to-quality bid that drives the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months and PAYEMS growth is negative over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if combined labor weakening does not precede lower 20-day Treasury yields in at least 60% of triggered instances, or if the median response is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in RSAFS indicates stronger consumption and nominal demand, which raises growth and inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month RSAFS increases are not followed by positive 20-day yield changes more often than negative ones, or if the conditional average response is below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained slowdown in GDPC1 growth alongside weak RSAFS signals demand softening and lower future inflation pressure, which pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below its prior 4-quarter average and RSAFS growth is also below its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to precede lower 60-day yield changes in at least 60% of cases, or if the median 60-day response is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO and HOUST together indicate stronger real activity and construction demand, which increases expected nominal growth and raises the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both increase on a 3-month moving-average basis
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous gains in industrial production and housing starts do not lead to higher 20-day yields in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the average response is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: During a goods-disinflation regime, stronger INDPRO growth reduces recession risk and lifts term premiums less than inflation worries, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 5 trading days when output remains weak and policy is unchanged.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of the 5-day yield response to INDPRO improvements is positive or indistinguishable from zero over any 2-year rolling window, or if stability in FEDFUNDS does not condition the effect.
